news.headline: Particles for Humanity and Hexagon Nutrition partner to combat vitamin A deficiency in Africa. Hexagon Nutrition will manufacture supplements.
news.summary: A collaboration has been formed between Particles for Humanity and Hexagon Nutrition Limited to combat vitamin A deficiency (VAD) in Africa. The partnership involves Hexagon Nutrition obtaining the rights to produce vital supplements. This initiative aims to tackle the prevalent issue of VAD in the region. The venture signifies a crucial step towards providing essential nutrition to combat health challenges. The collaboration between the organizations is expected to significantly impact the fight against VAD in Africa, where a large number of individuals are affected by this deficiency. The joint effort by the two entities highlights their commitment to addressing health issues and underscores the importance of partnerships in enhancing healthcare accessibility.

news.headline: NFT sales plummet 63% in Q1 2025 to $1.5B, but Pudgy Penguins, Doodles shine. CryptoPunks sales at $60m, BAYC down to $29.8m. Bitcoin NFT average price up to $633.24. Bitcoin NFT sales drop 79% to $291m in Q1 2025.
news.summary: Sales of non-fungible tokens (NFTs) took a sharp dive in the first quarter of 2025, plunging 63% year-over-year to $1.5 billion from $4.1 billion in 2024 according to CryptoSlam. Notably, while big players like CryptoPunks and Bored Ape Yacht Club saw significant drops in sales, collections like Doodles, Milady Maker, and Pudgy Penguins stood out with impressive gains amid the downturn. Pudgy Penguins led the way with $72 million in sales, a 13% increase from the previous year, while Doodles saw a jump to $32 million. Milady Maker had the biggest percentage increase at 58%. Additionally, Bitcoin NFTs saw a rise in average price to $633.24 despite a 79% drop in sales volume to $291 million in 2025 from $1.4 billion in 2024. This marks a significant shift in the NFT market landscape.

news.headline: SBC Medical Group Holdings Inc. reported $44 million in total revenues for Q4 2024, a 29% decrease from the previous year.

In Q4 2024, the company reported $44 million in total revenues, showcasing a significant 29% decline compared to the previous year. The gross profit for the same quarter stood at $34 million, marking a 22% decrease year-over-year. SBC Medical is a worldwide firm that owns, operates, and offers management services and products to cosmetic treatment facilities.

news.headline: Workplace wellness is vital for long-term success, replacing traditional leadership styles. Article on Investor's Business Daily.
news.summary: A shift in leadership style has moved away from the traditional authoritarian approach to one that prioritizes employee well-being. In the workplace, focusing on wellness can lead to healthier and more sustainable success. This change is reflected in a recent article titled "Wellness At Work Unleashes Healthier, Long-Term Success." The piece emphasizes the importance of creating a work environment that values the physical and mental health of employees. This approach has shown to result in increased productivity and overall success for businesses. Embracing wellness initiatives can ultimately lead to better outcomes in the long run.

news.headline: Lazarus Group paused operations in 2024 before setting up $1.4B Bybit hack. $1.34B stolen by N. Korean hackers in 2024. #CryptoHack #Blockchain
news.summary: North Korea-affiliated hackers appeared to have decreased their cyber operations in the latter part of 2024 before executing the largest crypto hack in history on Feb. 21, 2025, when Bybit lost $1.4 billion to the Lazarus Group. Blockchain analytics firm Chainalysis noted a drop in illicit North Korean activities post July 1, despite heightened attacks earlier that year. The hacking slowdown following a DPRK-Russian summit may have been a result of resource reallocation to the Ukraine war. The Lazarus Group laundered the stolen funds via THORChain in 10 days, with over 80% still traceable as experts work to recover the funds. The Bybit attack underscored the vulnerability of centralized exchanges to advanced cyber threats, reminiscent of similar hacks in the past. In 2024, North Korean hackers stole $1.34 billion across 47 incidents, doubling the amount from 2023 and constituting 61% of the total crypto stolen that year.])[Character count: 927]

G Mining Ventures reported Q4 2024 results:- Produced 40,147 oz of gold- Net income $47.6 million- AISC $862/oz Au sold- Cash and cash equivalents $141.2 million
news.summary: G Mining Ventures Corp. reported its fourth quarter and full-year financial and production results for 2024. In Q4 2024, they produced 40,147 ounces of gold, with gold sold totaling 39,938 ounces. They had an all-in sustaining cost of $862 per ounce of gold sold, resulting in a net income of $47.6 million. The company's adjusted EBITDA was $77.9 million, and they had cash and cash equivalents of $141.2 million. G Mining Ventures Corp. aims for future growth with acquisitions like Oko West, targeting over 500,000 ounces of annual production. Their Tocantinzinho Mine achieved 63,566 ounces in 2024, with a cash cost of $668 per ounce and AISC of $972 per ounce for the entire year. The company is focused on low-cost production and strategic investments for long-term value creation.

news.headline: ETH price drops 5% to $1,900 on Mar 28. Total crypto market cap down 2.67% to $2.78T. $97M in ETH long liquidations in 24 hrs. Trade tensions and bearish tech contribute to decline. Trump's tariffs spook investors, potential drop to $1,200 predicted. ETH down over 50% from 3-yr high.
news.summary: The price of Ether (ETH) dropped over 5% to $1,900 in the past 24 hours, in line with the broader cryptocurrency market decline to $2.78 trillion. Factors contributing to the drop include Trump's tariff tensions, long liquidations, and bearish technicals. Trump's announcement of a 25% tariff on automobile imports has spooked investors, leading to concerns of a market sell-off. Over $97 million worth of ETH positions were liquidated in 24 hours, with total liquidations across all assets reaching $353 million. ETH faces further decline towards $1,200 as technicals show a bearish pattern. The cryptocurrency has already dropped over 50% from its three-year high. Despite the bearish outlook, rising network activity and decreasing supply on exchanges may pave the way for a potential recovery to $2,500.

news.headline: So-Young International reported unaudited Q4 2024 total revenues of RMB369.2 million (US$50.6 million) with a net loss of RMB607.6 million, driven by a goodwill impairment charge. For FY 2024, revenues were RMB1,466.7 million (US$200.9 million) with a net loss of RMB589.5 million. So-Young declared a special dividend of US$0.03445 per ordinary share. Expect FY 2025 Q1 revenues of RMB280.0-300.0 million. Company aims for balanced growth in upcoming quarters.
news.summary: So-Young International Inc., a leading aesthetic treatment platform in China, reported unaudited financial results for Q4 2024 and fiscal year 2024. In Q4 2024, total revenues were RMB 369.2 million (approx. US$50.6 million), with a net loss of RMB 607.6 million (approx. US$83.2 million). The company facilitated RMB 356.6 million worth of medical aesthetic treatment transactions and had over 52,000 active users. For fiscal year 2024, total revenues were RMB 1,466.7 million (approx. US$200.9 million), with a net loss of RMB 589.5 million (approx. US$80.8 million). So-Young expanded to 19 aesthetic centers in nine major cities, with a special dividend declared of US$0.03445 per ordinary share. The company's Q1 2025 revenue outlook is between RMB 280.0 million (US$38.4 million) and RMB 300.0 million (US$41.1 million). The non-GAAP net loss for Q4 2024 was RMB 53.2 million (US$7.3 million).

news.headline: AMD CEO Lisa Su was named Time's CEO of the year for innovation in tech, including focusing on data centers and AI. In a 2018 interview, she called blockchain "very important." If $1,000 was invested in AMD back then, it could earn significantly now.
news.summary: Lisa Su, the CEO of Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (NASDAQ:AMD), has steered the company to become a major player in the chip industry, competing with giants like Nvidia and Intel. Recently, Time Magazine named Su as their CEO of the year, recognizing her leadership and focus on innovation in areas such as data centers and artificial intelligence. Su's insights on artificial intelligence, semiconductors, and other emerging technologies have garnered attention from industry observers and tech enthusiasts. Apart from these topics, Su's views on blockchains, seen as a revolutionary technology of the 21st century, have also intrigued many. In an interview with CNBC on April 26, 2018, Su emphasized the significance of blockchain technology. For more details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

Seritage specializes in owning and developing retail, residential, and mixed-use properties on a national scale.
